what_it_does: This file is the module constitution for the platforms registry: it declares that each external platform the Company mirrors is represented by one pure-data row file (platforms/<id>.py) containing a PLATFORM dict validated against PlatformEntry, and it documents the data-only discipline, discovery rules, three concrete platform instances, and downstream registry-driven consumers.
observations: Frontmatter tags it as a living Level-2 constitution for the platforms mirror-registry module.
Each platform is exactly one row file: imports plus a module-level PLATFORM dict, no definitions or classes.
The head_builder thunk was moved out of row files into platforms/_wiring.py to preserve row purity.
Platform id must match the filename after _/- normalization.
Pydantic Literal fields cause fail-loud validation for unknown adapter kinds, discovery source types, or invocation kinds.
Documents Instance #1 claude-code, Instance #2 gh-cli as the generalization proof, and Instance #3 codex-cli with a live in-fabric consumer.
Downstream consumers were lifted to be registry-driven rather than hardcoding claude-code.
cap:// capability wiring in Suite.__init__ is still scoped to claude-code only.
References introspection/AGENTS.md as the place where the engine mechanism is documented.
standouts: Strict DATA-ONLY discipline: no def/class, no if/for logic in a platform row file.
Generalization proof: gh-cli reuses the cli-help adapter with zero engine edits across a different CLI family.
Live consumer for codex-cli via ops/ledger_interpret_codex.py reading the registry row.
Build-time leak-gate grep ensures legitimate platform-name strings live only in platforms/ row files.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with type, register, module, aliases, tags, governs, relates-to, status.
Follows the addressable-by-file discipline: row id ↔ filename with hyphen/underscore normalization.
Uses Pydantic model_validate for fail-loud registry loading.
Separates pure data rows from bootstrap wiring in a _-prefixed module skipped by file discovery.
References related constitutions via [[...]] wiki-style links.
concerns: Known gap recorded but not fixed: codex-cli clap output puts flag descriptions on the next line, so descriptions come back empty under the current cli-help parser.
cap:// capability wiring remains claude-only, explicitly scoped as remaining work.
notes: This is the platforms module's constitution: it says platform rows are pure data, gives the rules for adding them, and walks through the three existing platform instances plus the remaining claude-only cap:// gap.
questions: What exactly do the R1/R2/R3/R5 classification bands mean in the classification output?
What is the full contract in contracts/platform_entry.py:PlatformEntry?
What does the leak-gate grep script look like and which strings does it consider platform names?
How is platforms/_wiring.py discovered and bound to the row without breaking row purity?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to define the Level-2 platform table as pure data and the rules for extending it; the file does exactly that, including concrete examples and known gaps.
apparent_themes: Row purity and separation of data from engine logic
intention_signals: The file is trying to make adding a new platform as cheap as dropping a pure-data row, while preventing the engine or adapters from hardcoding platform identities or classification logic.
novelty: The strict data-only row discipline combined with a build-time leak-gate grep that fails if platform-name strings appear outside platforms/; also the explicit generalization proof across three CLI families (Commander.js, Cobra, clap).
contribution: It contributes the prescriptive contract and documented instances for the platform registry, establishing how platform rows are shaped, validated, discovered, and consumed, and recording which parts are still claude-only.
summary_for_embedding: Module constitution for the platforms registry defining pure-data platform rows, validation rules, three instance platforms, and registry-driven consumers.
intention_for_embedding: To enforce a data-only, registry-driven platform table so new platforms can be added as row files without engine or adapter changes.
